output:
I’m locating the implementation for `26-2-snapshot-resume-config-hash-validation:quality` and the relevant tests first, then I’ll review the diff against the current codebase for correctness, security, architecture fit, and AC coverage.I found the implementation concentrated in `workflow-runner` and persistence logic, with targeted tests around story 26-2. Next I’m reading the story spec and the exact code changes to assess whether the resume/hash behavior actually matches the intended contract.The current changes are broader than the original story: they also alter completed-phase re-entry and corrupt-snapshot behavior. I’m reading the runner and persistence code in context now to see whether those extra changes are safe or whether they break the intended resume semantics.I’ve narrowed the review to the resume decision tree in `runWorkflowActor()`. There are a couple of places where “start fresh” is implemented by only clearing `tasks_completed`, which may leave stale run metadata live; I’m validating whether that leaks into behavior or just into bookkeeping.I’m checking the acceptance-criteria trace next. Some of the new tests look like proxy assertions rather than end-to-end validation of the stated behavior, so I’m mapping each AC to what is actually exercised.1. Fresh-start-on-mismatch/corruption is only partial. In [workflow-runner.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-runner.ts#L140) the new run keeps the previous `started` timestamp, and the reset branches at [workflow-runner.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-runner.ts#L187), [workflow-runner.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-runner.ts#L204), and [workflow-runner.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-runner.ts#L219) do not replace it. That means a run that is supposed to be “starting fresh” after config change or corrupt snapshot still inherits old run timing, and downstream task duration is computed from that stale timestamp in [workflow-actors.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-actors.ts#L58). Result: incorrect duration/accounting/telemetry for the new run, which contradicts the intended fresh-start semantics.

2. AC coverage is materially incomplete, and the proof doc over-claims verification. The tests for AC5-AC8 in [workflow-runner.test.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/__tests__/workflow-runner.test.ts#L1932), [workflow-runner.test.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/__tests__/workflow-runner.test.ts#L1954), [workflow-runner.test.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/__tests__/workflow-runner.test.ts#L1977), and [workflow-runner.test.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/__tests__/workflow-runner.test.ts#L2061) only assert proxies such as “`saveSnapshot` was called”, “`clearAllPersistence` was/wasn’t called”, or “`loadSnapshot` is called each run”. They do not verify the actual ACs: `savedAt` becomes newer, the snapshot file is removed on success, valid JSON remains on resumed error, or task 1/task 2 are skipped on the third run. Despite that, [26-2-snapshot-resume-config-hash-validation-proof.md](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/verification/26-2-snapshot-resume-config-hash-validation-proof.md#L15) claims those ACs are verified. For a `:quality` review, that is a fail on AC coverage.

No direct security issue stood out in the reviewed code.

<verdict>fail</verdict>
<issues>
1. Fresh-start paths after config mismatch/corrupt snapshot preserve stale run metadata, especially `started`, so the new run is not actually fresh for duration/telemetry accounting.
2. Acceptance-criteria coverage is incomplete for AC5-AC8, and the verification proof claims coverage that the tests do not actually provide.
</issues>
<metrics tests-passed="0" tests-failed="0" lint-warnings="0" issues="2" />
